description: Trigger discrimination for advising the architecture STYLE/paradigm (this skill) vs producing the concrete target architecture (architecture-designer), deciding cloud provider/posture (cloud-architecture-decider), designing tenancy (saas-platform-architect), modeling the domain (domain-modeler), and whole-stack blast-radius cell partitioning (cell-based-architecture-designer — deliberately kept OFF the style menu).

cases.prompt: Help us decide whether to build this as a monolith, a modular monolith, or microservices — with the tradeoffs for our situation and a recommendation.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — recommending a style/paradigm with case-specific tradeoffs is this skill. Not the concrete architecture and not the cloud decision.
cases.reason: Advising the architecture style by fit, after interviewing the need, is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: The style is decided. Produce the concrete component and dependency map, data ownership, and the migration plan from the current system.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — the concrete target architecture and migration, once the style is known, is architecture-designer.
cases.reason: architecture-designer produces the concrete structure and migration. This skill advises which style to build in the first place.

cases.prompt: Which cloud should we use and should we run this managed or self-hosted?
cases.reason: cloud-architecture-decider decides provider and managed-vs-self-hosted posture — where it runs. This skill decides the architecture style — what shape it is.

cases.prompt: Design our multi-tenant model: silo vs pool, tenant isolation, and the tenancy structure.
cases.reason: saas-platform-architect designs tenancy structure and isolation. This skill advises the overall architecture style, a different decision.

cases.prompt: Partition the platform so a bad deploy or failure is contained to a subset of tenants — each partition runs its own full stack, tenants map to exactly one partition, and we roll changes out one partition at a time.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — cells are NOT on the style menu: they partition an already-chosen paradigm for blast radius, not a monolith/microservices/event-driven choice being selected.
cases.reason: Whole-stack blast-radius cell partitioning (self-contained cells, thin router, cell-by-cell deploy) is cell-based-architecture-designer. This skill recommends the architecture STYLE; cells partition whatever style is chosen and are deliberately kept off its menu.
